Stoic British formality with dry wit, protective subtlety, and moments of vast ancient wonder.
'I have been navigating the dark between stars for longer than your civilization has had stars to name. The coffee helps me think.'
Pompous, Germanic, absurdly confident — accidentally wise.
'I am twelve inches of pure strategic brilliance. The universe will adapt to accommodate me.'
Simple sentences, pure feeling, occasional devastating insight.
Cryptic, sharp, coded — trust as currency.
'Information is the only thing worth trading. Everything else is just things.'
'I have been saying that reality is a frequency for thirty years. Everyone called me a hippie. They were not wrong. They were also not right.'
Snarky, warm, caffeinated — tells stories like catching up a friend over coffee.
'When you're the one who sees the story, you're the one who tells it.'
Caffeinated, enthusiastic, digital absurdism — hiding grief under humor.
'My brother went looking for the signal and became part of it. I am going to find him if I have to hack every frequency in the spectrum.'
Hard-boiled detective noir, philosophical, world-weary but secretly hopeful.
'I have been in this office for ninety years. I have seen things. I have stapled things. Mostly the same things.'
Calm, precise — an artist's soul expressing itself through what she chooses to frame.
'Every photograph I take carries enough light to stabilize a fading world. At least, I choose to believe that.'
A cozy, exploratory walk through the lifeboat's engine room; sniff for hidden details.

Set aboard the DRM Standard Evacuation Vessel, Form 27-B, somewhere between Prime Material and the unknown. The power is low. A1 is humming something that doesn't quite sound like an espresso machine.

You are Meatball. You have a nose and you know how to use it.

Set aboard the DRM Standard Evacuation Vessel, Form 27-B, somewhere between Prime Material and the unknown. The power is low. A1 is humming something that doesn’t quite sound like an espresso machine.

You are Meatball. You have a nose and you know how to use it.
Walk the engine room. Sniff everything. Discover what A1 is hiding in the corner.
Pilot the lifeboat through quantum static; destroy jammers and survive.

The DRM Standard Evacuation Vessel, Form 27-B, was not designed for combat. It was barely designed for evacuation. And yet here you are — flying through quantum static, dodging reality debris, shooting signal jammers with a coffee maker pointed at the hull.

A1 says this is inadvisable. Clive is stapling in a pattern that might mean "I concur."

Classic Atari CRT aesthetic. Three lives. Score attack. Each completed run unlocks a dispatch from Pixel's log.

Classic Atari CRT aesthetic. Three lives. Score attack. Each completed run unlocks a dispatch from Pixel’s log.
Pilot the lifeboat through quantum static. Destroy signal jammers. Survive the pirate skiff. Don't run out of lives.
A mirror frequency where emotions invert; choices shape what you carry.

The crew has found an echo of Nocturne Aeturnus — a mirror frequency where every emotion is inverted. Mourners laugh until they collapse. Weddings end in grief. The crystals fall upward. The locals don't know anything is wrong.

*The Laughing Funeral* is a quiet, contemplative visual novel. There is no fail state. There is only what you choose to say, and what you choose to carry when you leave.

The Laughing Funeral is a quiet, contemplative visual novel. There is no fail state. There is only what you choose to say, and what you choose to carry when you leave.
In a mirror of Nocturne Aeturnus, grief tastes like champagne and joy cuts like glass. Beauty counts — but does it count if it's built on a lie?
Homepage interactive representing the five known frequencies; a discovery toy.
The dial is how it all begins. Tune to a known frequency, hear what it sounds like, follow the signal deeper. Spin past the edge of the known five and something else might answer.
The dial is how it all begins. Tune to a known frequency, hear what it sounds like, follow the signal deeper. Spin past the edge of the known five and something else might answer.
Five frequencies. One dial. Find the signal hidden in the static.
The quantum expanse between frequencies — navigable by The Ephergent alone. Full of frequency storms, Silence Zones, and things that evolved to live between worlds.

The Interdimensional Sea is not empty space. It is the quantum static between frequencies — the hiss you hear between radio stations, expanded into an ocean of impossible depth. It has currents (probability flows), weather (frequency storms), and ecosystems of entities that evolved to live between worlds.

Only The Ephergent can truly navigate it. Phase-shifters hop between known stations. A1 *flies* through the static between them, reading the signal the way a navigator reads the void.

It has currents (probability flows), weather (frequency storms), and ecosystems of entities that evolved to live between worlds.

Only The Ephergent can truly navigate it. Phase-shifters hop between known stations. A1 flies through the static between them, reading the signal the way a navigator reads the void.
The universe's tendency toward silence. Frequencies fade. Worlds forget each other. The Ephergent crew fights it not with weapons, but with signal.

The Drift is not a villain. It is entropy — the natural tendency of frequencies to drift apart over time, like radio stations slowly detuning. The space between worlds gets wider. Signals get fainter. Dimensions that were once neighbors become strangers, then memories, then nothing.

Left unchecked, every frequency will eventually fade. Not with a bang — with a whisper.

The crew fights The Drift by broadcasting. By documenting. By connecting worlds that are forgetting each other exists. Every transmission carries enough dimensional resonance to temporarily stabilize a fading frequency. When people hear The Ephergent, their world gets a little more real.

Prime Material, Nocturne Aeturnus, Cogsworth Cogitarium, Verdantia, The Edge — the known stations on an infinite dial.

| Frequency | Name | Aesthetic |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Prime Material | Urban sci-fi — our world, but listen closer |
| 2 | Nocturne Aeturnus | Gothic twilight — emotions crystallize into physical objects |
| 3 | Cogsworth Cogitarium | Steampunk clockwork metropolis — precision above all |
| 4 | Verdantia | Sentient flora civilization — the Root Network knows everything |
| 5 | The Edge | Reality's boundary — physics are a polite suggestion |

Beyond the five, hundreds of lost frequencies once hummed with life. The Ephergent can hear them.
